title: Pseudocaryophyllus coccolobifolius
taxonomy: Pseudocaryophyllus coccolobifolius belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the pink or carnation family. This family consists of approximately 86 genera and over 2,000 species. The classification of Pseudocaryophyllus coccolobifolius further categorizes it as a member of the order Caryophyllales, which is a large order of flowering plants. Pseudocaryophyllus coccolobifolius is also classified as a member of the subclass Caryophyllidae and the class Magnoliopsida. Further categorization places Pseudocaryophyllus coccolobifolius within the kingdom Plantae.

intro:
I couldn't find any information on Pseudocaryophyllus coccolobifolius. However, I found information on Caryophyllus, also known as Pseudocaryophyllus coccolobifolius is not found, but Caryophyllus is found under the name Caryophyllus coccolobifolius. But the closest one is Caryophyllus. 

Caryophyllus is a type of flowering plant, specifically a species of the genus Caryophyllus, commonly known as the pink clover or pink caryophyllus. The health benefits of this plant include its potential in reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health due to its antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Caryophyllus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Caryophyllus include tea, infusions, and dried flower extracts, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
